paper.title: Development of a quality assessment model for dairy microflora in fresh cheese

mechanistic_equations.equation_description: The gamma concept expresses the effective growth rate as the product of the maximum growth rate and individual dimensionless gamma factors for each environmental variable, assuming multiplicative and independent effects.

parameters.name: maximum specific growth rate under optimal conditions

assumptions: Environmental factors have independent, multiplicative effects on growth rate.
Each gamma factor ranges from 0 (complete inhibition) to 1 (optimal conditions).

source_spans.text_context: The gamma concept expresses the effective growth rate as the product of the maximum growth rate and individual dimensionless gamma factors for each environmental variable, assuming multiplicative and independent effects.

mechanistic_equations.equation_description: Linear cardinal model describing the effect of water activity on microbial growth rate, scaling from zero at the minimum water activity to one at pure water.
assumptions: Linear relationship between a_w and growth rate fraction.
a_w = 1 corresponds to full growth potential from this factor.

source_spans.text_context: Linear cardinal model describing the effect of water activity on microbial growth rate, scaling from zero at the minimum water activity to one at pure water.

mechanistic_equations.equation_description: Reparameterized Gompertz equation describing sigmoidal microbial growth as a function of time, with biologically meaningful parameters for maximum growth rate, lag time, and asymptotic population.

parameters.name: log increase from initial to maximum

assumptions: Sigmoidal growth curve; applicable when growth data show a clear lag, exponential, and stationary phase.
Reparameterized form from Zwietering et al. (1990) for biological interpretability.

source_spans.text_context: Reparameterized Gompertz equation describing sigmoidal microbial growth as a function of time, with biologically meaningful parameters for maximum growth rate, lag time, and asymptotic population.

mechanistic_equations.equation_description: Dimensionless gamma factor representing the fractional effect of temperature on microbial growth rate, using the cardinal temperature model with minimum and optimal temperatures.
assumptions: Applicable for temperatures between T_min and T_opt.
Squared cardinal model following Ratkowsky or Zwietering convention.

source_spans.text_context: Dimensionless gamma factor representing the fractional effect of temperature on microbial growth rate, using the cardinal temperature model with minimum and optimal temperatures.

mechanistic_equations.equation_description: Relates microbial lag phase duration to storage temperature using an inverse power-law or exponential relationship, commonly used in shelf-life prediction models.
assumptions: Lag duration is inversely proportional to growth rate.
h0 is a strain-specific parameter representing the work needed to adapt to new conditions.

source_spans.text_context: Relates microbial lag phase duration to storage temperature using an inverse power-law or exponential relationship, commonly used in shelf-life prediction models.

mechanistic_equations.equation_description: Empirical square-root model relating the square root of growth rate to temperature, widely used in predictive microbiology as a simple secondary model.
assumptions: Linear relationship between sqrt(mu) and temperature in the suboptimal range.
T_min is a conceptual (extrapolated) value, not necessarily the observed minimum.

source_spans.text_context: Empirical square-root model relating the square root of growth rate to temperature, widely used in predictive microbiology as a simple secondary model.

mechanistic_equations.equation_description: Extended cardinal temperature model with minimum, optimum, and maximum temperatures, applicable across the full growth range including super-optimal temperatures.
mechanistic_equations.equation_latex: \gamma(T) = \frac{(T - T_{\max})(T - T_{\min})^2}{(T_{\mathrm{opt}} - T_{\min})\left((T_{\mathrm{opt}} - T_{\min})(T - T_{\mathrm{opt}}) - (T_{\mathrm{opt}} - T_{\max})(T_{\mathrm{opt}} + T_{\min} - 2T)\right)}
assumptions: Growth rate is zero at T_min and T_max, maximal at T_opt.

source_spans.text_context: Extended cardinal temperature model with minimum, optimum, and maximum temperatures, applicable across the full growth range including super-optimal temperatures.
